QF.id: VB Fund Distribution and Channel Management_23

question.text: The expense ratio of a Regular Plan is higher than a Direct Plan because the Regular Plan includes __________.

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** The only reason a Regular Plan has a higher expense ratio than a Direct Plan is the inclusion of distributor commission (trail commission). All other costs — fund management fees, custodian charges, audit fees — are the same in both plans.
**The Trap:** The fund manager is the same person managing the same portfolio. Custodian charges and SEBI fees are identical. Only the distributor commission creates the cost difference.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in Series V-A. This explains the fundamental cost difference between Direct and Regular Plans.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The commission difference is typically 0.5% to 1% per year. For a 10-year investment, this compounds significantly. Make sure you provide enough value to justify this cost.
